name: West Tisted
overview: West Tisted is a small estate village and civil parish in East Hampshire, just off the A32 about 6.7 miles north-west of Petersfield, within the South Downs National Park. It had a population of 165 at the 2011 census. Much of the parish belongs to the West Tisted Manor Estate (over 1,700 acres, farmed by R.S. Hill & Sons), and many homes are estate cottages. Its medieval church of St Mary Magdalene, reached over a moat bridge, dates from the 12th century and stands beside a yew thought to be over a thousand years old.
character: A tiny, quiet agricultural estate village surrounded by farmland and copse, deep in unspoilt South Downs countryside.
amenities: 12th-century Church of St Mary Magdalene and its ancient churchyard yew
The Angel pub nearby towards Privett
No village shop or post office - everyday shopping is in Alresford or Petersfield

commutes.notes: About 6.7 miles south-east to the nearest fast mainline station on the Portsmouth Direct line
For shops and the heritage Watercress Line; Alton station is a similar distance north-east
From Petersfield on South Western Railway; fastest services about 1h 2m

prices.summary: Because the manor estate owns much of the village, the private market is small and dominated by period and cottage-style homes, with very low transaction volumes; the South Downs National Park setting underpins values. See the live Rightmove sold-price page for current figures.

thingsToDo: The West Tisted Commons and Churches circular walk, taking in the medieval church and a tiny church in the woods
Walking and riding across the surrounding South Downs farmland and woodland
Seeing the ancient churchyard yew at St Mary Magdalene
placesToEat: The Angel, near the boundary with Privett
pros: Deeply rural, unspoilt South Downs National Park setting
A characterful country pub close by
cons: Very small private housing market, as the estate owns much of the village
No shop, school or station in the village
Car essential, with very limited public transport
whoItSuits: Buyers wanting a tiny, tranquil estate village deep in the South Downs, who value seclusion and walking over amenities.
